overview.title: Assessments & Data
overview.purpose: Track assessment types, reteach needs, and intervention connections.
overview.teacherReminder: Use data to guide the next small-group decision, not to overwhelm yourself.

assessmentTypes.title: DIBELS
assessmentTypes.whenToUse: Benchmark windows, progress monitoring, and reading intervention decisions.
assessmentTypes.teacherBrain: DIBELS helps identify who needs decoding, fluency, or comprehension support.

assessmentTypes.title: Open Court Weekly Assessments
assessmentTypes.whenToUse: End of weekly Open Court lesson cycle.
assessmentTypes.teacherBrain: Use weekly assessments to decide what needs reteaching before moving too quickly.

assessmentTypes.title: Open Court Unit 1 Assessment
assessmentTypes.whenToUse: End of Unit 1 or before reteach week.
assessmentTypes.teacherBrain: Unit assessments should show patterns across lessons, not just one bad day.

assessmentTypes.title: Writing Rubrics
assessmentTypes.whenToUse: During writing conferences and after published writing pieces.
assessmentTypes.teacherBrain: Use oral rehearsal before writing for students who struggle to get ideas on paper.

assessmentTypes.title: Science Checks
assessmentTypes.whenToUse: After science workbook lessons, labs, or videos.
assessmentTypes.teacherBrain: Science checks should connect vocabulary with hands-on understanding.

unit1Assessments.title: The Mice Who Lived in a Shoe

dataFlags.action: Picture cards + sentence frames + oral rehearsal
Main idea/details, cause/effect, text evidence frames
Model, sort, practice, apply in writing

progressMonitoring.notes: Use quick notes, not long forms.
Record reteach needs and group impact.
Use for Yellow and Blue group decisions.
Use for major group shifts and intervention planning.
